bodyHtml:
recon-skills is making waves in the Backend ecosystem this week, gaining 6 new stars. With a total of 267 stars on GitHub and 49 forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 267Stars
📈 +6/wkGrowth
🔀 49Forks
⚠️ 0Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in Python for maximum performance
  • Licensed under No license — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://github.com/uphiago/recon-skills
  • 🏷️ bug-bounty
  • 🏷️ cloud-security
  • 🏷️ cors-exploitation
  • 🏷️ firebase-hacking
  • 🏷️ hermes-agent

Why recon-skills Is Trending

The Backend landscape is constantly evolving, and recon-skills has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 6 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: 144 offensive security skills for recon and pentest. Field-validated techniques from 600+ targets across 45+ sectors. Updated with web enum, email sec, google dorks, cloud IAM, WordPress full compromise chains.

If you're working in Backend, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Practical Use Cases

Here are some common scenarios where recon-skills shines:

  1. New projects: Start your next Python project with this tool as a foundation
  2. Migration: Consider switching if your current solution is slowing you down
  3. Learning: Study the source code to understand modern Backend patterns

Quick Start

Get started with recon-skills in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/uphiago/recon-skills # Navigate to project cd recon-skills # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

recon-skills is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
